questions.question: Describe the primary difference between a relational data model and an entity-relationship data model. Provide examples of each.
questions.hint: Relational data models are based on tables and relationships, whereas entity-relationship data models are based on entities and attributes.

questions.question: What is normalization in database design, and how does it improve data integrity?
questions.hint: Normalization ensures that each piece of data is stored in one place and one place only, reducing data redundancy and improving data integrity.

questions.question: Explain the concept of data normalization and provide an example of a 3rd Normal Form (3NF) entity-relationship diagram.
questions.hint: A 3NF entity-relationship diagram is an example of a normalized database design where each non-key attribute depends on the entire primary key.
